name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
summary: Marjorie Taylor Greene has consistently demonstrated a pattern of promoting conspiracy theories, misinformation, and hate speech, often targeting minority groups, Democratic politicians, and individuals she disagrees with. She has expressed support for executing prominent Democrats, including Nancy Pelosi, Barack Obama, and Hillary Clinton, and has made derogatory comments about Muslims, Jews, and LGBTQ+ individuals. Greene has also promoted anti-vaccine misinformation, compared COVID-19 safety measures to the Holocaust, and advocated for Christian nationalism and "uniquely Anglo-Saxon political traditions." Her behavior has led to her removal from committee assignments and expulsion from the House Freedom Caucus, highlighting her extreme and divisive views.

entries.fact: Marjorie Taylor Greene began writing for the now-defunct American Truth Seekers, a conspiracy news website, which published 59 articles by Greene.
Marjorie Taylor Greene started writing for Law Enforcement Today, a pro-police fake news website, which featured 27 articles Greene authored.

Marjorie Taylor Greene visited the U.S. Capitol and congressional offices, where she posted livestream videos outside the offices of representatives, including Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Ilhan Omar, and Rashida Tlaib, making false claims and derogatory comments.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that representatives Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib were not 'official' representatives because they were sworn in to Congress on the Quran.
Marjorie Taylor Greene supported calls to execute prominent Democratic Party politicians, including Hillary Clinton and Barack Obama.

Marjorie Taylor Greene spoke at a gun rights rally in Villanow hosted by Silent No Longer, a group campaigning to make Walker County a Second Amendment sanctuary, and held up a banner for American Patriots USA, a far-right group.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Islamophobic, antisemitic, and white supremacist views, including the white genocide conspiracy theory.
Marjorie Taylor Greene held an AR-15 style rifle and warned antifa groups to 'stay the hell out of Northwest Georgia' in a video that Facebook took down for violating its terms of service.

Marjorie Taylor Greene amplified conspiracy theories that allege government involvement in mass shootings in the United States, implicate the Clinton family in murder, and suggest the attacks of 9/11 were a hoax.

Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a meme on Facebook depicting herself holding an AR-15 style rifle next to a collage of pictures of Ocasio-Cortez, Omar, and Tlaib, with the caption 'Squad's worst nightmare', which was later deleted by Facebook for violating its policies on inciting violence.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called Nancy Pelosi 'anti-American', a 'hypocrite', and a 'bitch' during her victory speech
Marjorie Taylor Greene made deceptive claims about her husband's voting record, suggesting voter fraud
Marjorie Taylor Greene equated the Democratic Party with Nazis and compared COVID-19 safety measures to the persecution of Jews during the Holocaust.
Marjorie Taylor Greene wore a face mask onto the House floor that read 'Trump Won', despite Trump having lost the 2020 presidential election
Marjorie Taylor Greene raised an objection to counting Michigan's electoral votes during the counting of electoral votes
Marjorie Taylor Greene refused to wear a face mask while sheltering in place during the riot at the US Capitol.
Marjorie Taylor Greene tweeted that Democrats must be held accountable for the political violence inspired by their rhetoric during the debate to impeach Trump.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely suggested that the rioters at the US Capitol were members of antifa dressed as Trump supporters.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was part of a group of Republican legislators who unsuccessfully challenged votes for Joe Biden during the 2021 United States Electoral College vote count.
Marjorie Taylor Greene filed articles of impeachment against President Joe Biden, citing alleged abuse of power as the reason for impeachment.
Marjorie Taylor Greene filed articles of impeachment alleging abuse of power against President Joe Biden.
The U.S. House of Representatives voted to remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from all committee roles in response to her endorsements of political violence.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Russian propaganda and praised its president Vladimir Putin during the Russian invasion of Ukraine.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was expelled from the conservative House Freedom Caucus after insulting fellow caucus member Congresswoman Lauren Boebert.
Marjorie Taylor Greene unsuccessfully attempted to oust Mike Johnson from his role as Speaker of the House of Representatives.

Marjorie Taylor Greene wrote on Twitter that children should not wear masks, calling mask recommendations 'unhealthy for their psychological, emotional, and educational growth' and 'emasculating' for boys.
Marjorie Taylor Greene refused to wear a mask in a secured room with other members of Congress during the storming of the Capitol.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced an article of impeachment against Biden, with no co-sponsors.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's threats against Democratic members of the House prompted Speaker Nancy Pelosi to speak of an 'enemy within the House of Representatives' and the need to increase security measures.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was removed from all committee assignments due to incendiary remarks she had made before her election.
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that COVID-19 vaccine passports might be 'Biden's mark of the beast', a reference to the Book of Revelation.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against a bill to reauthorize the National Marrow Donor Program.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that a bill to reauthorize the National Marrow Donor Program would fund aborted fetal tissue with taxpayer money.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was fined $500 for not wearing a mask on the House floor.

Marjorie Taylor Greene compared mask mandates to the Holocaust, saying that Pelosi's requirement for House members to wear masks was 'exactly the type of abuse' as Jews being 'put in trains and taken to gas chambers in Nazi Germany'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was fined a total of $48,000 for not wearing a mask on the House floor 20 times.

Marjorie Taylor Greene sent Biden a letter calling for an investigation into Fauci over his statements on the origin of SARS-CoV-2, the virus that causes COVID-19, and called COVID-19 a 'manufactured plague' caused by a 'Chinese-made virus'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene dismissed the COVID-19 variants, including the Delta variant, saying, 'no one cares'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promised that companies that complied with the House Select Committee on the January 6 Attack's request to retain phone records related to the attack would be 'shut down'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that Biden would be 'sending one of his police state friends to your front door' to inquire whether they had received a vaccine, and said, 'in the South, we all love our Second Amendment rights, and we're not really big on strangers showing up on our front door'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene admitted that COVID-19 was spreading, but falsely said that hospitals were not overwhelmed with afflicted patients, and added, 'we're human, we can't live forever, we're going to catch all kinds of diseases and illnesses and other viruses'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that the Plan B contraceptive 'kills a baby in the womb'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene spoke at a conference hosted by white supremacist Nick Fuentes, at which Fuentes called the January 6 attack 'awesome' and praised Putin and Hitler.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted the anti-vaccine film Died Suddenly, which promotes misinformation about COVID-19 vaccines and Great Reset conspiracy theories.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was expelled from the Freedom Caucus by a vote of its members after she called fellow caucus member Lauren Boebert a 'little bitch' during an argument on the House floor.
Marjorie Taylor Greene and Congresswoman Elise Stefanik introduced a pair of resolutions to expunge both of Trump's impeachments.
Marjorie Taylor Greene repeatedly refused to address Fauci as Doctor, questioned his medical licence and accused him of covering up COVID-19's origins during a congressional hearing.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a pair of resolutions to expunge the impeachments of Trump.
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Black Lives Matter activists to white nationalist participants at the August 2017 Unite the Right rally in Charlottesville, Virginia.
Marjorie Taylor Greene confronted Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg near the U.S. Capitol, questioning his gun control activism and calling him a 'coward' for ignoring her.
Marjorie Taylor Greene co-sponsored the Old Glory Only Bill, a bill to ban U.S. embassies from flying pride flags.

Marjorie Taylor Greene tried to block the Equality Act while it was being debated on the House floor, saying that it would 'destroy God's creation' and 'violate everything we hold dear in God's creation'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against a measure condemning the Myanmar coup d'état

Marjorie Taylor Greene supported a bill by Representative Mary Miller that would bar schools from allowing transgender students to use facilities such as bathrooms and locker rooms that correspond to their gender identity.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the verdict in the Derek Chauvin trial was a result of jury intimidation by Black Lives Matter, which she compared to the Ku Klux Klan.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called transgender women 'men playing dress up' and 'literally having an identity crisis', saying, 'They never will be [women], and I refuse to recognize them that way'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene stated she would kick out every Chinese person in the United States who is loyal to the Chinese Communist Party and impose strict tariffs on China
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against the bipartisan ALLIES Act, which would increase the number of special immigrant visas for Afghan allies of the U.S. military
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that any Republican who supported the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act was 'a traitor to our party, a traitor to their voters and a traitor to our donors'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that straight people would go extinct within less than two centuries due to LGBT-inclusive educators, calling them 'trans terrorists'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that 'an entire' pride month celebrating LGBT people 'needs to end'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene argued that Canada's proposed gun control laws would leave Canada 'weak and vulnerable to being invaded and easily taken over by another stronger country', such as 'Russia, who is very angry at America right now'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the July 4 Highland Park parade shooting was 'designed to make Republicans go along with more gun control' because the shooting occurred 'in a rich, white neighborhood'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced the 'Protect Children's Innocence Act', which would make giving gender-affirming care to transgender youth a felony punishable by up to 25 years in prison.
Marjorie Taylor Greene responded to California state senator Scott Wiener by saying that the Protect Children's Innocence Act would stop 'communist groomers' such as Wiener.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Russian propaganda, including claims that Ukraine is a Nazi state, and praised Vladimir Putin
Marjorie Taylor Greene sponsored a resolution to censure Democratic U.S. representative Rashida Tlaib for alleged 'antisemitic activity' and 'sympathizing' with Hamas
Marjorie Taylor Greene called for the lifting of U.S. sanctions on Russia
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed an amendment to a House foreign aid bill on Ukraine that would require all members of the House voting in favor of the bill to enlist in the Ukrainian military
Marjorie Taylor Greene was one of 18 Republicans to vote against admitting Sweden and Finland to NATO
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed a defense appropriations amendment that would end U.S. funding of Israel's Iron Dome
An updated version of Marjorie Taylor Greene's 'Protect Children's Innocence Act' passed the House by a vote of 216-211.
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed doubt that the perpetrator of the 2017 Las Vegas shooting acted alone and believed it was an attack on the right to bear arms.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the August 2017 Charlottesville white nationalist rally was an 'inside job'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed support for a conspiracy theory that a plane did not hit the Pentagon during the September 11 attacks.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the 2019 Christchurch mosque shootings in New Zealand were a false flag.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the 2018 Stoneman Douglas High School shooting in Parkland, Florida, was an organized false flag operation.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the 2012 Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a false flag operation.
Marjorie Taylor Greene wrote that Nancy Pelosi tells Hillary Clinton 'we need another school shooting' in order to persuade the public to want strict gun control.
Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a video promoting the antisemitic white genocide conspiracy theory that Zionists are conspiring to flood Europe with migrants to replace the native white populations.

Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a conspiracy theory about the Camp Fire, a deadly Californian wildfire, suggesting that it could have been caused by 'what looked like lasers or beams of blue light' from 'space solar generators' in a scheme involving California governor Jerry Brown, companies PG&E, Rothschild & Co, and Solaren.
Marjorie Taylor Greene indicated support for executing prominent Democrats
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg had been replaced in public appearances by a body double.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said Nancy Pelosi is guilty of treason and that treason is a crime punishable by death in a Facebook video.
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that Nancy Pelosi would either be killed or imprisoned for treason in Facebook live-streamed videos.
Marjorie Taylor Greene followed and taunted Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg, accusing him of using children for his cause.

Marjorie Taylor Greene live-streamed a video to her Facebook account in which she targeted and harassed Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg, calling him a 'coward' and falsely claiming he is funded by George Soros.

Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed support for the execution of leading Democrats, including Nancy Pelosi, Barack Obama, and Hillary Clinton, as well as support for the execution of FBI agents on her Facebook account.

Marjorie Taylor Greene uploaded a video to her YouTube account in which she targeted and harassed Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg, calling him a 'coward' and falsely claiming he is funded by George Soros.
Marjorie Taylor Greene told gun activist Chris Dorr that 'the only way you get your freedoms back is it's earned with the price of blood'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene agreed with a conspiracy theory known as Frazzledrip, which asserts that there is a video of Hillary Clinton and her assistant Huma Abedin murdering a child in a satanic ritual.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that Muslims who believe in Sharia law should not be in the U.S. government and that the Democratic Party is holding Black Americans as 'slaves'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called the Squad supporters of 'terrorism' and 'Hamas' who 'don't belong in Congress' and referred to them as the 'Jihad Squad'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene defended Christian nationalism and described herself as a 'proud Christian nationalist'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene planned to launch an America First caucus that aimed to promote 'uniquely Anglo-Saxon political traditions'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene denounced the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act, a civil rights and police reform bill, as an 'Anti-Police bill'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene told viewers that 'the only way you get your freedoms back is it's earned with the price of blood'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene attended the America First Political Action Conference, which was criticized for promoting white supremacy and anti-Semitism.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted a conspiracy theory that the U.S. government is planning to force Americans to eat fake meat grown in a 'peach tree dish' by Bill Gates.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that Democrats had designed the killing of Ayman al-Zawahiri to boost Biden in the midterms.
Marjorie Taylor Greene repeated her suggestion of a 'national divorce' between red states and blue states, which was condemned by Democrats and some Republicans.
Marjorie Taylor Greene posted online that 'yes they can control the weather … It's ridiculous for anyone to lie and say it can't be done'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene again indicated support for executing prominent Democrats
Marjorie Taylor Greene called Nancy Pelosi a 'b****' in her acceptance speech after winning the Georgia GOP primary
Marjorie Taylor Greene made disparaging remarks about Jews, Blacks, and Muslims

Marjorie Taylor Greene posted a picture of herself holding a gun next to a picture of 'The Squad', a group of Democratic congresswomen, with the caption 'Squad goals', which was removed by Facebook for inciting violence
Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'children should not wear masks', bucking the CDC's recommendation
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed the United States is experiencing an 'Islamic invasion into our government offices'
Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a video of a hotel room workout, citing 'Democrat tyrannical control'
A Marjorie Taylor Greene staffer threatened to have WRCB-TV reporter Meredith Aldis arrested after Aldis tried to ask Greene a question at a town hall event about her harassment of David Hogg

Marjorie Taylor Greene's personal Twitter account was locked for 12 hours for multiple violations of Twitter's civic integrity policy, based on her posts including false claims about voting fraud and statements blaming electoral officials in Georgia
Marjorie Taylor Greene's Twitter account was temporarily suspended for spreading COVID-19 vaccine misinformation
The full House voted to remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from her committee assignments, with 230 votes in favor and 199 against, and 11 Republicans joining all the Democrats
Marjorie Taylor Greene apologized for using the word 'retard' to refer to individuals with Down syndrome, stating it was a slang word and she was sorry if it was offensive to anyone
Marjorie Taylor Greene was barred from blocking anyone on her public Twitter or any other social media account while in office and was forced to pay $10,000 to cover legal fees for MeidasTouch
Marjorie Taylor Greene launched an 'America First' caucus pushing for 'Anglo-Saxon political tradition'
Marjorie Taylor Greene broke Georgia law by claiming homestead exemptions on two properties, when only one may legally be claimed

Common Cause filed an FEC complaint against Marjorie Taylor Greene for an alleged violation of McCain–Feingold campaign finance provisions, accusing her of implicit unlawful solicitation of unlimited contributions in an advertisement appearance
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against rewarding the Capitol Police with the Congressional Gold Medal for defending the Capitol Building during the 2021 United States Capitol attack
Marjorie Taylor Greene referred to the police guarding the Capitol building as 'gazpacho police', confusing the cold Spanish soup with the Gestapo
Marjorie Taylor Greene filed a federal lawsuit to block a law that allows a candidate to be removed from a ballot after review by an administrative law judge and the Georgia Secretary of State
Marjorie Taylor Greene accused Republicans who supported then-Supreme Court nominee Ketanji Brown Jackson of being 'pro-pedophile'

Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed without evidence that the Robb Elementary School shooter was transgender and had engaged in cross-dressing, and also claimed that the shooter was in the same Discord server as the teen accused of carrying out the 2022 Buffalo shooting
Twitter labeled two tweets by Marjorie Taylor Greene as violating their rules on hateful conduct for misgendering and deadnaming Assistant Secretary for Health Rachel Levine

Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized the presence of the Georgia Guidestones, a megalithic granite monument, and said that the ecumenical texts inscribed on it represented a nefarious future of 'population control' as envisioned by the 'hard left'
Marjorie Taylor Greene targeted and misgendered congresswoman Sarah McBride in a series of tweets, labeling her a 'groomer'
Marjorie Taylor Greene sparked controversy when she celebrated the death of Pope Francis, tweeting 'Today there were major shifts in global leaderships. Evil is being defeated by the hand of God.'
Marjorie Taylor Greene harassed Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez's office during a 2019 Capitol Hill visit
Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'our elections should be decertified'
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted to overturn election results
Marjorie Taylor Greene refused to wear a mask during the Capitol insurrection
Marjorie Taylor Greene filed articles of impeachment against Biden
Marjorie Taylor Greene tried to force Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib to retake their oaths on a Bible
Marjorie Taylor Greene was a moderator of a Facebook group featuring death threats and racist memes
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced an anti-trans bathroom bill in Congress to stop 'radical gender ideology'
Rep. Greene aggressively confronted Rep. Ocasio-Cortez, causing New York congresswoman to raise security concerns
Marjorie Taylor Greene defended Capitol rioters in a House floor speech
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Democrats to Nazis, saying Nazis were the 'National Socialist party' of their time like the Democrats are now
Marjorie Taylor Greene apologized for comparing vaccine rules to the Holocaust
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted no on a bill to award the Congressional Gold Medal to January 6 police officers
Marjorie Taylor Greene downplayed speaking at a conference founded by white nationalist Nick Fuentes
Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'we should be Christian nationalists' in an interview
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted for a resolution to expunge the January 13, 2021, impeachment of President Donald John Trump
Marjorie Taylor Greene spread conspiracies about the Las Vegas shooting
Marjorie Taylor Greene spread conspiracies about Charlottesville and 'Pizzagate'
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against a measure condemning the military coup in Myanmar.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called COVID-19 vaccine passports 'Biden's mark of the beast'
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed bills to fire Fauci and ban vaccine passports
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Black Lives Matter protests to Ku Klux Klan tactics
Marjorie Taylor Greene questioned the jury's verdict in the Derek Chauvin trial
Marjorie Taylor Greene was fined $500 for repeatedly going maskless on the House floor
Marjorie Taylor Greene called Nancy Pelosi 'mentally ill'
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared House mask mandates to the Holocaust
Marjorie Taylor Greene argued that Covid-19 escaped from a Wuhan lab and stated she doesn't 'believe in evolution, I believe in God'
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that teaching about gay people is 'emotional child abuse'
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Biden's vaccination push to Nazis
Marjorie Taylor Greene laughed off a question about children dying of Covid
Marjorie Taylor Greene said she would deport Chinese people if she could.
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested Southerners could 'welcome' Biden's 'police state friends' with guns
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed hospitals weren't reaching capacity due to the Covid surge and said 'we can't live forever'
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against speeding up visas for Afghans fleeing the Taliban.
Marjorie Taylor Greene accrued at least $48,000 in fines for ignoring the House mask mandate
Marjorie Taylor Greene gave her definition of a woman, which sparked controversy
Marjorie Taylor Greene groundlessly claimed that straight people face extinction within 150 years because of LGBTQ+ education
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that Pride Month 'needs to end'
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against a resolution to support Finland and Sweden joining NATO.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a bill to make gender-affirming care for transgender youth a felony
Marjorie Taylor Greene hurled a homophobic slur at a Bay Area representative
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against a bill directing the removal of troops from Syria.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted to cut US funding for Israel.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that Muslim congresswomen should retake their oaths on the Bible
Marjorie Taylor Greene described climate change as a 'scam' and fossil fuels as 'amazing'
Marjorie Taylor Greene was involved in the formation of the 'America First' caucus, which aimed to protect 'Anglo-Saxon political traditions'
Marjorie Taylor Greene called the Squad the 'Jihad Squad' and said they should be removed from Congress
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared COVID-19 vaccine efforts to the Holocaust
Marjorie Taylor Greene stated that she does not believe in evolution, referring to it as 'so-called science'
Twitter suspended Marjorie Taylor Greene over 'misleading' Covid posts
Marjorie Taylor Greene debated 'national divorce' between Democrat and Republican states with Steven Bannon
Marjorie Taylor Greene called for a national divorce between Republican and Democratic states
Marjorie Taylor Greene said the GOP 'should be Christian Nationalists' party
Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'they can control the weather'; it's not clear who she means
The House passed a bill criminalizing gender-affirming care for minors, which Marjorie Taylor Greene supported
